[PUBLISHED DATE]: Aug. 5, 2013
[TITLE]: Pants off in Times Square
[METADATA/INTRO]: Revelers wearing only their underwear dance in New York's Times Square in an attempt to break a Guinness Book of World Records record for "the most amount of people gathered in their underwear in the Times Square section of New...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Revelers wearing only their underwear dance in New York's Times Square in an attempt to break a Guinness Book of World Records record for "the most amount of people gathered in their underwear in the Times Square section of New York," Aug. 5, 2013. 
 Organizers were hoping to get 2,271 people to strip down to their underwear - the current record is 2,270. 
 Dot Portnoy, 83, of Florida, dances with her husband, while attempting to break the Guinness Book of World Records record for "the most amount of people gathered in their underwear in the Times Square section of New York," Aug. 5, 2013.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2016-06-29
[TITLE]: The Wild & Wacky: Photographs from NYC's LGBTQ Pride Weekend (NSFW) | HuffPost Contributor
[METADATA/INTRO]: NYC's LGBTQ Pride weekend is by far one of my favorite events to photograph. Between the loud excitement of the parade and the colorful display of eccentric characters, the event attracts a special kind of crowd: a big ball of...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: NYC's LGBTQ Pride weekend is by far one of my favorite events to photograph. Between the loud excitement of the parade and the colorful display of eccentric characters, the event attracts a special kind of crowd: a big ball of love and happiness ready to strip half naked, vogue, twerk, "yaaaasss" all day and—to my advantage—have their photo taken in all sorts of compromising positions. 
 There has always been a fuzzy place in my heart for the gay community and I find great comfort in the weird, the wild and the wacky: a lot of which overwhelms the West Village on this particular weekend.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: POC @ Pride NYC — Arcade Project
[METADATA/INTRO]: POC @ Pride NYC Renowned photographer Ruben Natal-San Miguel took to the streets this Pride month and focused his lens on the color and diversity of New York’s LGBTQIA+ community....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: POC @ Pride NYC 
 Renowned photographer Ruben Natal-San Miguel took to the streets this Pride month and focused his lens on the color and diversity of New York’s LGBTQIA+ community. 
 Outlandish outfits, extravagant floats and spontaneous dance parties are mixed in with the political components of this annual civil rights march celebrating LGBTQ+ equality and attended by people from all over the world. The event commemorates the first gay rights march, held in NYC on June 28, 1970, to celebrate the first anniversary of the Stonewall Uprising. 
 New York City Dyke March 
 The New York City Dyke March is a protest march, not a parade.
